steps.title: Locate the known points from the figure
steps.content: From the figure, $P$ is at $(1, 0)$ on the positive $x$-axis (at $0^{\circ}$) and $T$ is at $(-1, 0)$ on the negative $x$-axis (at $180^{\circ}$). The center $O$ is at the origin. Points $Q$ and $R$ lie on the circle, with $Q$ in the first quadrant and $R$ in the second quadrant.

steps.title: Find the position of Q using angle POQ
steps.content: Since $\angle POQ = 30^{\circ}$ and $P$ is at $0^{\circ}$, point $Q$ is located $30^{\circ}$ counterclockwise from $P$. Therefore, $Q$ is at $30^{\circ}$ from the positive $x$-axis.

steps.title: Find the position of R using angle ROT
steps.content: Since $\angle ROT = 30^{\circ}$ and $T$ is at $180^{\circ}$, and $R$ appears above $T$ in the second quadrant, point $R$ is located $30^{\circ}$ counterclockwise from $T$. Therefore, $R$ is at $180^{\circ} - 30^{\circ} = 150^{\circ}$ from the positive $x$-axis.

steps.title: Calculate angle QOR and convert to radians
steps.content: The central angle $\angle QOR$ is the difference between their positions: $$\angle QOR = 150^{\circ} - 30^{\circ} = 120^{\circ}$$ Converting to radians: $$120^{\circ} \times \frac{\pi}{180^{\circ}} = \frac{120\pi}{180} = \frac{2\pi}{3}$$ That confirms C is correct.
